steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: For coordinate-circle questions, read the center and radius from the equation or graph the circle as a check. For diagram facts, mark equal radii, diameters, tangents, and arcs before doing algebra.

steps.title: Recognize how the two arcs partition the circle
steps.content: The minor arc MN (the shorter path from M to N) and the major arc MPN (the longer path from M through P to N) together form the complete circle. These two arcs share endpoints M and N but cover all other points on the circle exactly once—no overlap, no gaps.

steps.title: Add the arc lengths to find the circumference
steps.content:
Since the two arcs together make up the entire circle:
$$\text{Circumference} = \text{arc } MN + \text{arc } MPN = 39 + 195 = 234 \text{ cm}$$
That confirms D is correct.
